hunyuan-image3-base-tokenizer-guard.name: hunyuan-image3-base-tokenizer-guard
hunyuan-image3-base-tokenizer-guard.description: Guard HunyuanImage3 __init__ against Base model tokenizer missing extended ratio tokens (<img_ratio_33..36>) when MoE/Instruct config is mistakenly used.
hunyuan-image3-base-tokenizer-guard.body:
## Root cause
`vllm_omni/model_executor/models/hunyuan_image3/hunyuan_image3.py` lines ~1560-1563 in `HunyuanImage3ForConditionalGeneration.__init__`:

```python
ratio_33 = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("<img_ratio_33>")
ratio_36 = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("<img_ratio_36>")
self._ratio_other_slices = [(ratio_33, ratio_36 + 1)]  # None + 1 → TypeError
```

The Base checkpoint (`tencent/HunyuanImage-3.0`) tokenizer only has `<img_ratio_0>` through `<img_ratio_32>`. The extended tokens `<img_ratio_33>`–`<img_ratio_36>` are Instruct-only. Tencent's reference code guards this with a `model_version == "HunyuanImage-3.0"` check at `tokenization_hunyuan_image_3.py:613`.

## Fix
1. Add a `None` guard with a clear error message before computing `ratio_36 + 1`.
2. Document that Base uses `hunyuan_image3_dit.yaml` (single-stage DiT) and Instruct uses `hunyuan_image_3_moe.yaml` (two-stage AR→DiT).

## Workaround
Use `vllm_omni/deploy/hunyuan_image3_dit.yaml` for the Base model (confirmed working).
